snippet.title: ONE ANTENNA SHIELDED NINE HOUSES FOR TWENTY YEARS ⚡📡🏠
snippet.description: Retired ham radio operator Gene Whitfield took down his forty-foot antenna in Pensacola last October. Nobody on Oleander Drive thought twice about it. Then three homes were struck by lightning within seven weeks. Insurance adjusters flagged the cluster. A meteorological engineer confirmed that Whitfield's antenna — properly grounded with copper rods since 2003 — had functioned as the neighborhood's de facto lightning rod for two decades. County records showed zero residential strikes on the block during that entire period. Whitfield's neighbors pooled $4,100 to buy him a new antenna. He installed it the following Saturday. It's taller than the original by six feet.

snippet.title: British Scouts Dug Up a Buried German Radio — The Frequency Range Changed
snippet.description:
A British patrol dug up a buried German radio eight days after D-Day. The casing was intact. The oilcloth was still damp. And the frequency dial didn't end where it should have.
What engineers found inside that dial range forced a complete rewrite of the Allied jamming plan for Normandy — because for eight days, the Germans hadn't been hiding from Allied signals. They'd been listening to them.
This is the story of one buried radio, one third terminal, and nine metres of copper wire that nearly handed the Germans a map of the entire Allied electronic network in France.

#WW2 #DDay #Normandy #WorldWarII #MilitaryHistory #SignalsIntelligence #WW2History #SecretHistory #ForgottenHistory

snippet.title: I Powered a 20W HF Radio With What!
snippet.description:
tester to prove what was actually happening at the power input. Spoiler: it worked, and the numbers genuinely surprised me. In this video I walk through exactly how to use an inexpensive USB tester to measure the real current draw of your ham radios, including handhelds, QRP HF rigs, and digital hotspots. If you have ever wondered how long your battery will actually last in the field, or whether your power bank can really push enough juice for HF, this is the test bench setup that answers it without guesswork. I also put the Ugreen power bank through its paces with the FX4CR, including charging behavior, voltage stability under transmit load, and what happens when you push it harder than the spec sheet suggests it should handle. For SOTA activators, POTA operators, EmComm folks, and anyone running portable or off grid, this kind of real world measurement is the difference between a confident deployment and a dead radio at the worst possible moment. Whether you are putting together a go kit, planning your next POTA activation, or just trying to squeeze every watt out of a small battery, the USB tester earns its place in the toolbox fast. And the Ugreen power bank earns a spot in the field bag.

snippet.title: Does everyone get nervous before their first transmission? #hamradio #questions
snippet.description:
Free Video Course - The Top 5 Beginner Ham Radio Frustrations (and How To Overcome Them): https://hamradiolaunchpad.com/free

Feeling anxious about your first ham radio transmission? Totally normal — almost every new operator does. The good news: it fades fast. Start with a local repeater, have your first line scripted, and remember nobody on the other end is judging you. By your third QSO you'll wonder why you were ever nervous. Push the PTT — it gets easier every time.

#HamRadio #AmateurRadio #VHF #HamRadioTips #NewHam

snippet.title: The America 250 POTA Rove begins! Plus a special announcement.
snippet.description:
Video date: 6/22/26
Our America 250 POTA Rove and Battlefield Rove begins in earnest tomorrow. My wife and I are joining our passions, hers for history and mine for POTA, into one epic celebration of America's 250th Birthday.  In this video I provide a list of the sites we'll visit and attempt to activate, along with the announcement of a certificate that will be awarded to the Top 5 Hunters over the entire trip.  The Hunters (5) who show up the most in the combined logs of all activations will receive a really nice certificate to document their success.
This is going to be fun.  Please subscribe to the channel to follow along and use Ham Alert to know when we are on the air. 
Most activations will be on 20m and 40m.
I hope you'll follow along and hunt me as we celebrate America.
Paul
KJ5GKK

snippet.title: Ptcb certification practice test pdf 2026 Pass Your Pharmacy Exam Fast
snippet.description:
Ptcb certification practice test pdf 2026 Pass Your Pharmacy Exam Fast

Are you preparing to take the pharmacy technician certification exam in 2026 and feeling completely overwhelmed by the sheer volume of information you need to memorize? You are not alone. With the recent updates to the testing standards, securing your pharmacy tech license requires more than just memorizing drug names. It requires a strategic approach to practice questions, top 200 drugs, pharmacy math, and patient safety protocols. This video breaks down the exact concepts you need to understand to pass your test on the very first attempt. We dive deep into real world scenarios and the exact phrasing you will see on the official test so you are never caught off guard. Finding accurate study materials can make or break your test day experience. Many candidates spend months reading thick textbooks but fail because they do not practice with realistic test questions. By working through these specific practice scenarios, you train your brain to quickly identify trick answers and narrow down the best possible choice. We will cover federal requirements, medication safety, quality assurance, and the notoriously difficult pharmacy calculations that trip up so many test takers. You will learn the easiest ways to convert measurements, calculate dosages, and handle day supply math without panicking. We know that everyone learns differently, which is why we offer comprehensive resources tailored to your specific needs. We also provide full length premium practice tests, detailed study guides, and one on one tutoring sessions to help you master the material faster. Whether you are struggling with pharmacology, law, or complex compounding formulas, our expert team is here to guide you step by step until you achieve your certification. Passing the pharmacy technician exam in 2026 is about studying smarter, not harder. You need to focus heavily on the top prescribed medications, their generic and brand names, standard classifications, and common side effects. We recommend creating a consistent study schedule where you tackle a new core competency each day. Focus on understanding the why behind every answer rather than just memorizing the final result. When test day arrives, pacing yourself is critical. You will have a limited amount of time to answer a large number of multiple choice questions, so learning how to quickly eliminate incorrect options is a skill we focus on heavily in this guide. Let us talk about the specific domains you need to master. Medications make up a huge portion of your score. You must be able to recognize drug interactions, therapeutic equivalents, and proper storage conditions. We will walk you through exactly how to group medications by their prefixes and suffixes so you can easily identify a drug class even if you have never seen the specific medication before. This simple hack saves countless hours of brute force memorization. Furthermore, order entry and processing are vital skills that test makers love to evaluate. You need to know how to read a prescription, identify missing information, and understand standard medical abbreviations. Patient safety and quality assurance are more heavily emphasized in 2026 than ever before. You will encounter questions about error prevention strategies, proper hygiene standards, and handling hazardous materials. Your journey to becoming a certified pharmacy technician starts with the right preparation. Commit to mastering these practice concepts, utilize all the knowledge shared here, and you will walk out of that testing center with a passing score.

#PTCBExam #PharmacyTechnician #PTCB2026 #PTCE #PharmacyTech #PTCBPrep

snippet.title: The USB C Trick That Powers My Radio
snippet.description:
Portable power is the foundation of any serious off grid ham radio kit, and in this video I show how a single Anker 737 power station, a 30W folding solar panel, and a simple USB C trigger cable can run an entire field communications setup with zero access to the grid.
I walk through powering a USB C handheld radio directly from the Anker 737, running a Starlink Mini for internet anywhere, and then using a USB C to DC trigger cable to power a QRP HF radio in the field. To close the loop, I demonstrate recharging the Anker 737 from the 30W solar panel so the entire kit becomes self sustaining for extended deployments, field day operations, POTA activations, SOTA, and emergency communications scenarios.
This setup is ideal for amateur radio operators, preppers, off grid travelers, overlanders, and anyone building a portable communications go kit. The Anker 737 offers enough capacity to keep multiple devices running for hours, and the USB C trigger cable approach eliminates the need for bulky linear power supplies or dedicated LiFePO4 battery boxes for low power HF work.
If you have been looking for a clean, lightweight, and modern way to power your radios in the field, this combination is hard to beat.

🔹Links mentioned in the video

Want your raspberry pi to play nice with your ham radio? Are you into digital modes like FT8, JS8Call, FLDIGI, Winlink, APRS, Digipeaters and others? Each week I put out new content showing you exactly what steps to take to get the latest ham radio software on your raspberry pi. Whether you just want to ragchew or are seriously into emergency preparedness, emcomm, or ARES and want to learn all you can about using the raspberry pi to drive your ham radio, this channel has something for you. Be sure to click the subscribe button and ring the bell before heading off. That way you will get notified of all future videos.

If you have been looking for a clean, lightweight, and modern way to power your radios in the field, this combination is hard to beat.

snippet.title: Every Cell Service Backup a beginner can buy, Ranked
snippet.description:
Most beginners pick the wrong cell service backup. Here’s a ranking of all the standard options that work for beginners.

There are a lot of things that can cause a cell service outage. From power outages to bugs in the code, your cellphone is reliant on a system that is ultimately out of your hands. We rely on cell service for our every day lives, and when a disruption occurs, it can interrupt our sense of what is normal.

A lot of things that can cause a cell service outage are also the same things that can necessitate reaching out to family. Emergencies of all kinds can take down a network, but the same emergency might also be what caused you to reach for your phone in the first place. Without a backup in mind, there’s no way to reach loved ones when it counts… or is there?

snippet.title: Ape Answers 022: Man Made RF Noise Interference
snippet.description:
Reduce RF noise interference by understanding how your HF ham radio antenna setup interacts with man-made electrical noise. Learn to improve your reception.

This video examines the physics behind RF noise interference and why specific antenna designs struggle more than others in noisy environments. If you are struggling with a high antenna noise floor, this breakdown clarifies how different antenna types react to local interference sources. It is designed for radio operators looking to optimize their station performance by understanding the root causes of signal degradation.

We focus on the practical differences between vertical and horizontal antennas, specifically regarding their susceptibility to man-made interference. By analyzing the interaction between these designs and common noise sources, you will gain a clearer understanding of how to manage your signal to noise ratio effectively. Choosing the right antenna is often the most critical step in clearing up your receiver.

Subscribe for weekly antenna physics and radio performance breakdowns, and comment below with your biggest challenge when dealing with local interference.

snippet.title: TESTING DIFFERENT TELEGRAPH CONTROLLERS
snippet.description:
A close-up technical demonstration showcases a radio communications enthusiast testing three distinct designs of specialized dual-lever telegraph paddles (or iambic keys).

These high-speed manual instruments are engineered to help amateur radio operators fluidly transmit Morse code messages by generating continuous streams of automated dits and dahs with minimal finger movement. The first controller features a minimalist, classic polished brass mechanism mounted on a heavy, premium green marble base. The operator then transitions to a more industrial, tactical black key with thick, robust mechanical linkages. Finally, a highly modernized, precision-machined aluminum "K7OQC" dual-lever paddle mounted on a sleek translucent red resin disc is tested. Each test illustrates how responsive, high-precision metallic contacts allow for tight spacing adjustments and quick tactical bursts, giving operators maximum speed and crisp feedback when sending signals to a receiver.

snippet.title: Claims Of FCC Crackdown On Repeater Jammers, More Data Transmissions Over GMRS, ARRL Ham Field Day
snippet.description:
Like my shirt? Get yours here! --- https://amzn.to/3vc1Whf
In this week's Radio-Dork News:
GME, an Australia based company that has only recently entered the GMRS radio market in the United States has filed a waiver request with the FCC, asking that the FCC allow additional location data transmissions for two of their proposed new GMRS radios. The exemptions from FCC Part 95 rules, if permitted would allow digital data transmissions, location sharing with brief text messages, and more frequent automated updates on vehicle-mounted and handheld GMRS radios, with the aim of increasing safety for off-road and remote users in areas lacking cellphone service, citing prior similar approvals granted for Midland and Garmin.  Public Comments on the waiver request must be submitted no later July 17, 2026,

Ham radio operators from around the country will be lured out of their basements this weekend for the the 2026 Annual ARRL Field Day, the largest amateur radio on-air event in North America, demonstrating the importance of ham radio and its ability to provide critical communications during times of trouble. More than 50,000 licensed amateur radio operators are expected to attend events located across the country to practice their emergency response abilities and to demonstrate to the public how critical ham radio operators are during emergencies and disasters. You can download the 41 page Rules and information book for this year's Field Day at the ARRL. ORG website

WHC Connect, a facebook based GMRS repeater group has made a public facebook post claiming that the FCC has taken action on their behalf, after complaints of misuse of the WHC Connect GMRS repeater. The Facebook post, dated June 16, claims that an unmarked FCC vehicle was dispatched to the area to monitor usage of their repeater located in Fayette County, Ohio. The post goes on to claim that due to minimum radio traffic and poor transmission quality at the time, the FCC was not able to quote "prosecute" anyone, but warns that the area of the repeater abusers was tracked and added that the FCC has stated that they will return to both prosecute and seize the radio equipment of anyone that continues to interfere with the repeater.  The post also points out that fines and jail time are additional options, even though FCC fines are extremely rare and no repeater jammer has been jailed in at least the last 20 years. The post by WHC Connect also encourages anyone with any evidence of illegal repeater-use to share it with the FCC via the FCC's Consumer Complaints Webpage.

And that is all of the radio-dork news that is fit to print. Join me again next week, or sooner, when I may, or may not, do this again.

snippet.title: Is the Used Ham Radio Market Dead? The Truth is Brutal.
snippet.description:
The United States has roughly 737,000 active amateur radio licenses on file, and that number keeps growing. On paper, the hobby has never looked healthier. But operators who have tried to sell used HF rigs recently are telling a different story. The Collins, Drake, and Heathkit equipment that once held its value at Dayton Hamvention flea markets is no longer moving the way it used to.

What changed was not one thing. It was several forces hitting the market in sequence over the span of about a decade, each one compounding the damage from the last. Some of them came from overseas. Some came from inside the hobby itself. And at least one of them is not something the market can recover from.

This is the full story of what happened to the used ham radio market, told in the order it unfolded.

snippet.title: The end of maximum pressure? Trump’s deal could give Iran billions | Eye for Iran 109
snippet.description:
The new US-Iran Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) could unlock tens of billions of dollars for Tehran and fundamentally reshape Iran's economic and political future. Host Negar Mojtahedi speaks with former US Treasury sanctions official Max Meizlish, economist Mohamad Machine-Chian, former US military intelligence officer Michael Pregent, and Iran analyst Omid Memarian to explain what the agreement means for sanctions, the IRGC, Qatar, China and post-Khamenei Sr. Iran.

In this episode:

Max Meizlish, a former Treasury Department Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) official, explains why General License X could provide "unconditioned, unrestricted sanctions relief," potentially giving Tehran access to tens of billions of dollars. He argues the IRGC stands to receive a significant financial windfall and warns that China could emerge as one of the agreement's biggest beneficiaries.

Mohamad Machine-Chian examines whether the agreement can stabilize Iran's economy. He explains why FATF restrictions, Iran's isolated banking system and entrenched corruption remain major obstacles, while arguing the new funds could help Iran's Central Bank avoid a deeper inflation crisis.

Former US military intelligence officer Michael Pregent argues Qatar wielded extraordinary influence during the negotiations, warning that "Every Qatari negotiated outcome is a failure for US national security." He explains why he believes the agreement echoes the concessions made during the JCPOA and what it could mean for future US policy toward Iran.

Omid Memarian, Director of Communications at DAWN, says the Islamic Republic is entering an "identity crisis" following the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. He predicts the next battle will unfold inside the regime itself, as competing factions fight to define Iran's future and the next phase of the Islamic Republic.

Topics covered:
Trump's Iran MOU explained
General License X
Iran sanctions relief
Iran oil exports
The IRGC and Iran's economy
Frozen Iranian assets
Qatar's role in the negotiations
China's influence in Iran
FATF and Iran's banking system
Post-Khamenei Iran
Internal power struggles inside the Islamic Republic
US-Iran relations
Middle East geopolitics

If you enjoy in-depth reporting and expert analysis on Iran and the Middle East, subscribe to Eye for Iran and turn on notifications so you never miss a new episode.

snippet.title: You're Not In My Log | First West Virginia POTA
snippet.description:
My first West Virginia activation from US-6993 Bear Rock Lakes Wildlife Management Area while heading to Dayton Hamvention on Wednesday, May 13, 2026, was an absolute 40m frenzy, testing the Yaesu FT-891 to its absolute limits during a 20-minute non-stop pileup. Even with the intensity, I was able to recognize a callsign that would never get into my log. Watch to see how I and the gear handled the chaos!

Thank you to the all of the hunters and fellow activators for the contacts!

Search for videos of your contacts at https://ve2zdx.com/toolkit/youtube/videos/K3JRZ

http://dx-code.com.

snippet.title: ＜DayDay.＞SOTA HANAMURA(花村想太)「Lose」テレビ初 生披露【アーティストLIVE】
snippet.description:
Da-iCEのボーカル【SOTA HANAMURA(花村想太)】が2026年6月16日(火)放送の『DayDay.』に生出演。「Lose」をテレビ初 生披露しました。

【SOTA HANAMURA(花村想太)】
ボーカル兼パフォーマー。4オクターブの高音ボイスが魅力で、楽曲制作も多数行う。作詞した『CITRUS』では「第63回日本レコード大賞」を受賞。ソロバンドプロジェクト「Natural Lag」(2020年～)、YouTuberヒカルとのユニット「UPSTART」(2021年～)でも活動中。東宝ミュージカル「JERSEY BOYS」では主演を務め、「令和4年度(第77回)文化庁芸術祭賞」新人賞を受賞。2026年6月3日(水)にPre Debut Album『ASCEEENSION』を発売。

snippet.title: Ham Radio Hobby Has No Bottom! #hamradio #tips #tutorial
snippet.description:
Free Video Course - The Top 5 Beginner Ham Radio Frustrations (and How To Overcome Them): https://hamradiolaunchpad.com/free

Amateur radio has no ceiling — the deeper you go, the more it opens up. New ham or veteran, there's always another band to explore, another mode to try, another contact to chase. That's the magic of this hobby: it scales with your curiosity. Set up your rig, get on the air, and see where it takes you. The hobby rewards people who stick with it.

snippet.title: 전기기능사 공부, 사실 전기기사의 시작입니다
snippet.description:
전기기능사를 공부하고 계신 분들 중에는
“전기기사나 전기산업기사까지 이어서 준비할 수 있을까?”
궁금해하시는 분들이 많습니다.

사실 전기기능사와 전기기사·전기산업기사는 과목 연계가 매우 깊습니다.

전기기능사의 3과목인
① 전기이론
② 전기기기
③ 전기설비는

전기기사와 전기산업기사에서 다음과 같이 더 깊고 넓게 확장됩니다.

전기이론 → 전자기학 + 회로이론
전기기기 → 전기기기
전기설비 → 전력공학 + 전기설비기술기준

즉, 전기기능사에서 배우는 내용은 단순히 기능사 시험에서 끝나는 것이 아니라,
기사·산업기사 공부의 중요한 기초가 됩니다.

기능사 공부를 하면서 과목 간의 연결 구조를 이해해두면,
나중에 전기산업기사나 전기기사에 도전할 때 훨씬 수월하게 공부할 수 있습니다.

전기기능사를 준비하는 모든 분들이
이번 영상을 통해 더 큰 목표를 바라보는 계기가 되었으면 좋겠습니다.

천천히 가도 괜찮습니다.
중요한 것은 포기하지 않고 꾸준히 이어가는 것입니다.

여러분의 합격과 성장을 진심으로 응원합니다.

#전기기능사 #전기기사 #전기산업기사 #전기이론 #전기기기 #전기설비 #회로이론 #전자기학 #전력공학 #전기설비기술기준 #전기자격증 #전기공부 #다산에듀
